concentration.headline: Strong urban primacy — capital drives the country
concentration.body: Jakarta dominates Indonesia's urban hierarchy with 32.1% of tracked urban population across 296 cities. The gap between Jakarta and the second city is substantial, reflecting a historically centralised national structure.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: France, Mexico, South Korea

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Jakarta & Bekasi
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Jakarta and Bekasi (20 km apart, combined population 36,137,053). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 21 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Indonesia shows clear urban primacy: Jakarta accounts for 32.1% of tracked population. The gravity network (Jakarta → Bekasi, avg 21 km) reinforces this — economic mass concentrates in and around the dominant city, with secondary cities playing supporting rather than competing roles.
